title: Torenia crustacea
taxonomy: Torenia crustacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It belongs to the genus Torenia, which comprises annual and perennial herbs. The plant is native to tropical Asia and is commonly found in humid and shaded areas. Torenia crustacea is further classified under the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that include mint, figwort, and snapdragon. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
uses: Torenia crustacea is primarily grown as an ornamental plant due to its vibrant flowers, but there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. More research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Torenia crustacea. Its traditional use in folk medicine is largely anecdotal and not well-documented.
